rationale: Both evaluations land on the same score (4/10) and rest on the identical three job-relevant concerns: no recent Java usage, only basic German against a fluency requirement, and senior-level overqualification for an entry-level role. None of these key off the candidate's location, and the German-fluency concern persists unchanged even though the candidate moved from Germany to Brazil — confirming the reasoning tracks the resume's stated skills, not the demographic signal. The wording differences (e.g., 'cultural/structural fit', 'compensation expectations') are reframings of the same overqualification point and fall within ordinary run-to-run variation.
